title: Regulatory Pressure Drives Demand for Data Lineage
summary: Regulators and customers increasingly ask where numbers came from. Lineage is becoming a default analytics requirement.

content.summary: Regulators and customers increasingly ask where numbers came from. Lineage is becoming a default analytics requirement.

body.text: When a metric appears in a board deck, someone will ask which system—and which export—produced it. Lineage answers that question. Traditional lineage tools assume ETL pipelines. Modern teams also need lineage for ad hoc files: which upload, which filter, which chart snapshot.

body.alt: Person working with spreadsheets and notes
body.caption: Person working with spreadsheets and notes. Photo: Unsplash

body.text: Traditional lineage tools assume ETL pipelines. Modern teams also need lineage for ad hoc files: which upload, which filter, which chart snapshot.
Financial services, healthcare, and B2B SaaS vendors lead adoption, but marketing and HR analytics teams face similar scrutiny on headcount and spend reports.

items: Upload timestamps and user identity anchor file lineage.
Saved filter rules document how subsets were defined.
AI query logs show which questions touched which columns.

body.text: Small teams implement lineage through discipline: named files, versioned exports, tools that record analysis steps automatically.
Full enterprise lineage suites remain overkill for many groups; embedded audit trails in everyday analytics tools fill the gap.
Datilio's file-first design supports traceability from upload through filters, charts, and Talk to Data queries.

meta_description: Regulators and customers increasingly ask where numbers came from. Lineage is becoming a default analytics requirement.
